findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated metadata and styling text not present in the source PDF, including a new HTML title and theme/CSS branding.
findings.evidence: HTML contains '<title>Converted Document</title>' and CSS comments/text such as 'Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS' plus imported font/theme variables. The PDF page only shows the agenda content and does not contain these textual elements.

findings.description: The PDF agenda is a three-column aligned list without visible header labels, but the HTML table adds a fabricated header row with column names not shown in the PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML table includes header cells 'Time', 'Topic', and 'Presenter'. The PDF shows agenda entries like '10:00-10:05 Review Agenda and Brief Introductions (Neelufa Sarwar)' but no explicit table header row.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is not fully faithful: the HTML uses two top-level heading levels for what appears in the PDF as centered title lines with emphasis, and it introduces multiple h1/h2 structural semantics not clearly present in the source.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses '

AGENDA

' and '

NEUSE RIVER BASIN HYDROLOGIC MODEL ... MEETING

'. In the PDF, these are centered text lines with bold styling, not necessarily distinct heading levels; additionally the HTML contains nested
elements, suggesting structural generation rather than faithful document hierarchy.
